tier_justification: Tier 1: one work-unit (a shape guard added to post_evals::smoke_verify), no irreversible/outward surface (no publish, deploy, migration, data deletion, external send). Above tier 0 because no existing test covers the malformed-.evals shape at the smoke_verify gate — the goal state is not already covered by an existing verify-criterion.

evals.assert: smoke-verify ACCEPTS a valid array artifact whose only eval is agent-run (empty scripted indices legitimately re-executes nothing and returns 0 — the regression the guard must not break).
evals.cmd: cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && f=$(mktemp) && printf '%s' '{"tier":1,"evals":[{"id":"A","mode":"agent-run","priority":"P0"}]}' > "$f"; bash scripts/post_evals.sh smoke-verify "$f" "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >/dev/null 2>&1; rc=$?; unlink "$f"; exit $rc
evals.negative_control: cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && f=$(mktemp) && printf '%s' '{"tier":1,"evals":42}' > "$f"; bash scripts/post_evals.sh smoke-verify "$f" "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >/dev/null 2>&1; rc=$?; unlink "$f"; exit $rc
evals.expected: cmd exits 0 (agent-run-only valid array accepted). negative_control exits nonzero (scalar .evals=42 refused by the fixed guard). On unfixed main the negative_control would exit 0 — that is the fail-open, and it is what the guard closes.

evals.assert: smoke-verify ACCEPTS a valid array with a real scripted eval (re-executes it), and REFUSES a string-typed .evals.
evals.cmd: cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && f=$(mktemp) && printf '%s' '{"tier":1,"evals":[{"id":"S","mode":"scripted","priority":"P0","cmd":"true","negative_control":"false"}]}' > "$f"; bash scripts/post_evals.sh smoke-verify "$f" "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >/dev/null 2>&1; rc=$?; unlink "$f"; exit $rc
evals.negative_control: cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && f=$(mktemp) && printf '%s' '{"tier":1,"evals":"notanarray"}' > "$f"; bash scripts/post_evals.sh smoke-verify "$f" "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >/dev/null 2>&1; rc=$?; unlink "$f"; exit $rc
evals.expected: cmd exits 0 (valid array with a scripted eval accepted and re-executed). negative_control exits nonzero (string .evals refused by the fixed guard).

evals.assert: smoke-verify REFUSES an object-typed .evals — the sneakiest non-array shape, where to_entries on an object otherwise yields keys and mis-iterates.
evals.cmd: cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && f=$(mktemp) && printf '%s' '{"tier":2,"evals":[{"id":"S","mode":"scripted","priority":"P0","cmd":"true","negative_control":"false"}]}' > "$f"; bash scripts/post_evals.sh smoke-verify "$f" "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >/dev/null 2>&1; rc=$?; unlink "$f"; exit $rc
evals.negative_control: cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && f=$(mktemp) && printf '%s' '{"tier":1,"evals":{"E1":{"mode":"scripted"}}}' > "$f"; bash scripts/post_evals.sh smoke-verify "$f" "$(git rev-parse HEAD)" >/dev/null 2>&1; rc=$?; unlink "$f"; exit $rc
evals.expected: cmd exits 0 (valid array accepted at tier 2). negative_control exits nonzero (object .evals refused by the fixed guard — the guard keys on type==array, not on to_entries succeeding).

evals.assert: The post_evals test suite passes AND contains a regression test exercising a non-array .evals at smoke_verify.
evals.cmd: cd "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)" && bash hooks/scripts/tests/post_evals.test.sh >/dev/null 2>&1 && grep -qiE 'non-array|not an array|evals.*(scalar|malformed|42|notanarray)' hooks/scripts/tests/post_evals.test.sh
evals.expected: cmd exits 0 (suite green AND a malformed-.evals regression test present). negative_control exits nonzero (bogus marker absent).
